skill.name: zentao-task-planner
skill.description: Plan ZenTao tasks and perform ZenTao operations including task creation, work hour logging, and task completion.
skill.summary: AI-powered ZenTao task planning and automation skill for project management workflows.
skill.author: AriesYB

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate ZenTao task management integration skill. Static analysis flagged 160 potential issues, but evaluation confirms these are mostly false positives. The skill legitimately accesses .env files for credential storage (required for ZenTao authentication) and makes HTTP requests to a user-configured ZenTao instance (core functionality). No prompt injection or malicious intent detected.

high_findings.title: Environment Credential Storage
high_findings.description: Skill reads ZenTao credentials from .env files. Credentials are required for ZenTao API authentication and stored locally per user configuration.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill legitimately requires credentials to authenticate with ZenTao. The .env file loading is intentional and necessary for the integration to work. No evidence of credential exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: External HTTP Requests to User-Configured Endpoint
medium_findings.description: Skill makes HTTP requests to a ZenTao instance URL configured by the user in environment variables.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: HTTP requests are made to ZENTAO_BASE_URL which users configure themselves. This is the core functionality of the skill - connecting to the user's own ZenTao instance. No unauthorized data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives - Chinese Text Strings Misidentified as Crypto
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged Chinese task type names (e.g., '产品方案设计') as 'weak cryptographic algorithms' due to character pattern matching. No actual cryptographic operations are performed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Detailed code review confirms the TASK_TYPE_DICT is a simple string mapping dictionary with no cryptographic operations. The scanner's pattern matching on Chinese characters caused false positives.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives - Markdown Code Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: Backtick syntax in markdown documentation files (references/*.md) was flagged as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are markdown inline code formatting, not actual shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All backticks in markdown files are documentation examples showing script usage syntax. No shell execution or eval() calls were found in these files.

low_findings.title: High File Entropy in Markdown Files
low_findings.description: Several markdown files show high entropy readings due to dense Chinese technical documentation, not encrypted or obfuscated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: High entropy is caused by Chinese character encoding, not binary or encrypted content. All files are standard UTF-8 encoded text files.

content.user_title: Plan and Automate ZenTao Task Management
content.value_statement: Managing ZenTao tasks manually is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates task planning, creation, work hour logging, and completion through AI-powered workflows.
actual_capabilities: Query and plan workdays to determine optimal task scheduling windows
List active, assigned, finished, and closed ZenTao tasks
Batch create tasks from standardized TSV task plans
Record work hours and mark tasks as finished
Automatically close finished tasks with completion comments
Parse and validate task information against ZenTao requirements
limitations: Requires local ZenTao instance with valid API credentials
Cannot modify existing tasks, only create and complete new ones
Task types must be pre-configured or mapped to ZenTao standard types
May require user confirmation before executing write operations

use_cases.title: Weekly Task Planning for Development Teams
use_cases.description: Development leads use this skill to plan weekly tasks for their team members. The AI helps allocate work evenly across workdays, splitting large features into appropriately-sized tasks.

use_cases.title: Automated Work Hour Logging
use_cases.description: Team members can quickly log completed work hours and mark tasks as finished without navigating through the ZenTao web interface.

use_cases.title: Batch Task Creation from Spreadsheets
use_cases.description: Import task lists from Excel or CSV files to quickly create multiple ZenTao tasks with proper formatting and validation.

prompt_templates.title: Plan My Week
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan my tasks for [this week/next week]. I have story IDs 103615, 103620, and 103621 to work on. Allocate tasks evenly across workdays.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to plan upcoming work

prompt_templates.title: View Active Tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me my active tasks in ZenTao

prompt_templates.title: Create Tasks from Plan
prompt_templates.prompt:
Create the following tasks in ZenTao:
[Copy your TSV task plan here]

prompt_templates.title: Finish Tasks by Date
prompt_templates.prompt: Mark all my tasks completed on [date] as finished with [X] hours logged

output_examples.input: Plan my tasks for next week with story 103615
output_examples.output: Generated task plan with 8 tasks distributed across Monday-Friday, each task under 8 hours with proper deadline dates.
List of 5 active tasks with IDs, names, deadlines, and estimated hours remaining.
Summary showing 3 tasks successfully closed with timestamps.

best_practices: Always preview task plans before execution to catch validation errors
Keep task estimates between 2-8 hours for accurate tracking
Use standardized task type names to ensure proper ZenTao categorization
anti_patterns: Do not create tasks without user confirmation
Do not assume dates without explicitly asking the user
Do not skip task validation even for urgent requests

faq.question: What credentials do I need to configure?
faq.answer: You need ZENTAO_BASE_URL, ZENTAO_ACCOUNT, ZENTAO_PASSWORD, and ZENTAO_NAME in your .env file.

faq.question: Can I modify existing tasks?
faq.answer: No, this skill only creates new tasks and completes or closes existing ones. It does not support modifying task details.

faq.question: How do I preview tasks before creating them?
faq.answer: Run scripts with the preview mode (without --execute flag) to see what would be created without making changes.

faq.question: What is the task plan format?
faq.answer: Tasks are provided as TSV (tab-separated values) with columns: Name, Story ID, Task Name, Type, Estimate (hours), Start Date, End Date.

faq.question: How does task type mapping work?
faq.answer: The skill can auto-map common types like 'backend coding' to ZenTao standard types, or you can provide a custom username mapping JSON file.

faq.question: Is my ZenTao password stored securely?
faq.answer: Credentials are stored locally in your .env file and only used for API authentication. The skill does not transmit passwords to third parties.
